text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the structure of existing code without changing how it works. When you refactor, you focus on making the code cleaner, more readable, and easier to maintain. For example, you might rename unclear variables, break long functions into smaller pieces, or remove duplicate logic. Refactoring does not add new features or fix bugs—it only improves the internal quality of the code. Over time, poorly structured code becomes "technical debt," which slows down development and increases errors. Regularly refactoring helps reduce this debt, making it easier for teams to understand and modify the code in the future.
